sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.63 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a group of automorphisms of a vector space over a field of characteristic zero, and suppose that all elements of$G$are unipotent, with uniformly bounded unipotency indices. Must such a group be locally finitely stable? { B.\,I.\,Plotkin} Yes, it must, which follows from (H.\,Heineken, , { 13} (1962), 29--37). Moreover, such a group is even finitely stable, as follows from (E.\,I.\,Zel'man...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
